hook: AI gave me a 5-paragraph essay when I needed a bullet point.
caption:
AI gave me a 5-paragraph essay when I needed a bullet point.

This is the thing nobody talks about. It's not that AI is wrong. It's that AI doesn't know *you*.

Some people think in frameworks. Give them a 3-step process and they're off.

Some people need the context first — the why before the what — or nothing sticks.

Some people want brutal brevity. One line. Done.

Some people need to see the counterargument before they'll trust the answer.

AI defaults to one mode: confident, structured, medium-length, slightly formal. That's the average of everyone. Which means it's perfect for no one.

Every time you rephrase your prompt trying to get a better response? That's you manually doing what a personal AI profile should do automatically.

You shouldn't have to train the AI every single conversation. It should already know you want the short version. The direct version. The version that skips the recap at the end.

That's the whole idea behind BootFile — map how you actually think, so the AI responds like it knows you. Not like it just met you.

Link in bio if you're tired of editing AI outputs back into your own voice.
